LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY'S SPECIAL AND GENERAL DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN ITS ENTIRETY WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFFS MUST PLEAD AT LEAST THE FOLLOWING: (1) THE TIMELY FILING OF A SUFFICIENT GOVERNMENT CLAIM (FOWLER V. HOWELL (1996) 42 CAL.APP.4TH 1746, 1752); (2) A STATUTORY BASIS OF LIABILITY. GOV. CODE 815(A). CIVIL CODE 1714 STANDING ALONE DOES NOT PROVIDE A STATUTORY BASIS FOR LIABILITY OF A PUBLIC ENTITY. ZELIG V. COUNTY OF LOS ANGELES (2002) 27 CAL.4TH 1112, 1132. IF PLAINTIFFS WISH TO PLEAD STATUTORY LIABILITY BASED ON GOV. CODE 835, PLAINTIFFS MUST PLEAD THE EXISTENCE OF SOME DEFECT IN THE PROPERTY ITSELF AND THE EXISTENCE OF A CAUSAL CONNECTION BETWEEN THE DEFECT AND PLAINTIFFS' INJURY. ZELIG, 27 CAL.4TH AT 1138. PLAINTIFFS MAY ALSO PLEAD ANY OTHER STATUTORY BASIS FOR LIABILITY THAT THEY BELIEVE SUPPORTS THE IMPOSITION OF A LEGAL DUTY ON THE HOUSING AUTHORITY. ID. AT PP. 1138-1139. THE STATUTORY BASIS FOR LIABILITY MUST BE PLED WITH PARTICULARITY, LOPEZ V. SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT (1980) 40 CAL.3D 780, 795, AND (3) FACTS SUFFICIENT TO SHOW THAT PLAINTIFFS TERRILLYNN CAIN, CHER'REL CAIN, AND ALONZO SCOTT, JR. HAVE STANDING UNDER CCP 377.60. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE CURTIS KARNOW; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS MCCORMACK BARON RAGAN MANAGEMENT SERVICES, INC. AND HAYES VALLEY APARTMENTS LIMITED PARTNERSHIP'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND ON THE ISSUE OF STANDING. PLAINTIFFS MUST PLEAD FACTS SUFFICIENT TO SHOW THAT PLAINTIFFS TERRILLYNN CAIN, CHER'REL CAIN, AND ALONZO SCOTT, JR. HAVE STANDING UNDER CCP 377.60. ON THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, TO THE EXTENT THAT PLAINTIFFS SEEK TO PLEAD A WRONGFUL DEATH CAUSE OF ACTION PREMISED ON A BREACH OF CONTRACT, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. A WRONGFUL DEATH ACTION MUST BE BASED ON A TORTIOUS ACT. BARRETT V. SUPERIOR COURT (1990) 222 CAL.APP.3D 1176, 1183. HOWEVER, A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR WRONGFUL DEATH MAY BE BASED ON A PROPERLY PLED BREACH OF THE WARRANTY OF HABITABILITY BECAUSE SUCH ACTIONS MAY SOUND IN TORT. STOIBER V. HONEYCHUCK, (1980) 101 CAL.APP.3D 903, 917. BUT THE DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND BECAUSE PLAINTIFFS HAVE FAILED TO PLEAD SUFFICIENT FACTS TO CONSTITUTE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR TORTIOUS BREACH OF WARRANTY OF HABITABILITY. PLAINTIFFS MUST PLEAD, AMONG OTHER THINGS, FACTS IN SUPPORT OF THESE ELEMENTS: (1) THERE WAS A MATERIALLY DEFECTIVE CONDITION AFFECTING HABITABILITY; (2) DEFENDANT HAD ACTUAL OR CONSTRUCTIVE KNOWLEDGE OF THE DEFECTS; (3) THE EFFECT OF THE CONDITION ON HABITABILITY WAS NOT APPARENT ON A REASONABLE INSPECTION; (4) THE DEFENDANT WAS GIVEN NOTICE WITHIN A REASONABLE TIME AFTER THE TENANT DISCOVERED THE BREACH; AND (5) THE DEFENDANT WAS GIVE A REASONABLE TIME TO CORRECT THE DEFECT WHILE THE DECEDENT REMAINED IN POSSESSION. 12 B. WITKIN, SUMMARY OF CALIFORNIA LAW, REAL PROP., 627(10TH ED. 2005); QUEVADO V. BRAGA (1977) 72 CAL.APP.3D 1, 7-8. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE CURTIS KARNOW; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED GRANTING DEMURRER FILED BY DEFENDANT SPECIAL TACTICS AND RESPONSE INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-22-2012 Continued To Nov-07-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-06-2012 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY'S SPECIAL AND GENERAL DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained in its entirety with leave to amend. Plaintiffs must plead at least the following:
(1) The timely filing of a sufficient government claim (Fowler v. Howell (1996) 42 Cal.App.4th 1746, 1752);
(2) A statutory basis of liability. Gov. Code 815(a). Civil Code 1714 standing alone does not provide a statutory basis for liability of a public entity. Zelig v. County of Los Angeles (2002) 27 Cal.4th 1112, 1132. If Plaintiffs wish to plead statutory liability based on Gov. Code 835, Plaintiffs must plead the existence of some defect in the property itself and the existence of a causal connection between the defect and Plaintiffs' injury. Zelig, 27 Cal.4th at 1138. Plaintiffs may also plead any other statutory basis for liability that they believe supports the imposition of a legal duty on the housing authority. Id. at pp. 1138-1139. The statutory basis for liability must be pled with particularity, Lopez v. Southern California Rapid Transit District (1980) 40 Cal.3d 780, 795, and
(3) Facts sufficient to show that Plaintiffs Terrillynn Cain, Cher'rel Cain, and Alonzo Scott, Jr. have standing under CCP 377.60. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge Curtis Karnow; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/CK

=(302/CK)

Defendants Mccormack Baron Ragan Management Services, Inc., Hayes Valley Apartments Limited Partnership'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-06-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. BEAU BURBINCLE OF THE LEWIS BRISBOIS LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 438-6693 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS MCCORMACK BARON RAGAN MANAGEMENT SERVICES, INCORPORATED;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants Mccormack Baron Ragan Management Services, Inc. AND Hayes Valley Apartments Limited Partnership'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint is Sustained with leave to amend on the issue of standing. Plaintiffs must plead facts sufficient to show that Plaintiffs Terrillynn Cain, Cher'rel Cain, and Alonzo Scott, Jr. have standing under CCP 377.60. On the second cause of action, to the extent that Plaintiffs seek to plead a wrongful death cause of action premised on a breach of contract, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. A wrongful death action must be based on a tortious act. Barrett v. Superior Court (1990) 222 Cal.App.3d 1176, 1183. However, a cause of action for wrongful death may be based on a properly pled breach of the warranty of habitability because such actions may sound in tort. Stoiber v. Honeychuck, (1980) 101 Cal.App.3d 903, 917. But the demurrer to the second cause of action is sustained with leave to amend because Plaintiffs have failed to plead sufficient facts to constitute a cause of action for tortious breach of warranty of habitability. Plaintiffs must plead, among other things, facts in support of these elements: (1) there was a materially defective condition affecting habitability; (2) Defendant had actual or constructive knowledge of the defects; (3) the effect of the condition on habitability was not apparent on a reasonable inspection; (4) the Defendant was given notice within a reasonable time after the tenant discovered the breach; and (5) the Defendant was give a reasonable time to correct the defect while the Decedent remained in possession. 12 B. Witkin, SUMMARY OF CALIFORNIA LAW, Real Prop., 627(10th ed. 2005); Quevado v. Braga (1977) 72 Cal.App.3d 1, 7-8. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge Curtis Karnow; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported
=(302/CK)
